tasks.title: Task 1: Sign in to AWS Management Console

steps.text: Click on the Open Console button, and you will get redirected to AWS Console in a new browser tab.

On the AWS sign-in page, Leave the Account ID as default. Never edit/remove the 12 digit Account ID present in the AWS Console. otherwise, you cannot proceed with the lab. Now copy your User Name and Password in the Lab Console to the IAM Username and Password in AWS Console and click on the Sign in button.
On the AWS sign-in page,
Leave the Account ID as default. Never edit/remove the 12 digit Account ID present in the AWS Console. otherwise, you cannot proceed with the lab.
Now copy your User Name and Password in the Lab Console to the IAM Username and Password in AWS Console and click on the Sign in button.
Once Signed In to the AWS Management Console, Make the default AWS Region as US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1.
Note : If you face any issues, please go through FAQs and Troubleshooting for Labs.
In this task, you will create a DynamoDB table named "WhizOrderTable" with specific configuration settings. Follow the steps below:
Make sure you are in the US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1 Region.
Navigate to DynamoDB page by clicking on the Services menu at the top. DynamoDB is available under the Database section.
Once you have selected Dynamodb, click on the Create Table
Table details: Table name : WhizOrderTable Partition Key : UserName (String) sort key : OrderID (String)
Table details:
Table name : WhizOrderTable
Partition Key : UserName (String)
sort key : OrderID (String)
Task 2 : Create DynamoDB Table
Keep all other settings as default. Click on Create Table.
Once the table is created, click on the table you have created and select ExploreTable Items tab to review the default setting of the table.
Task 2 : Create DynamoDB Table
In this task, you will create items in the DynamoDB table named "WhizOrderTable" with specific attribute values. Follow the steps below:
On the right side panel, click on the Explore Items under Tables.
Now select the table you have created i.e WhizOrderTable and click on Create Item.
Task 3 : Create Item
Once you select the create item, you’ll see UserName and OrderID, but we need 2 more attributes in our table. Click on Add new attribute and then select String from the drop down menu.
Give the attribute name as ReturnDate
Click on Add new attribute again and select String from the drop down menu.
Now Give the attribute name as UserAmount
Now enter the values as shown below: UserName : Enter HarryPotter OrderID : Enter 20160630-12928 ReturnDate : Enter 20190705 UserAmount : Enter 142.23
Now enter the values as shown below:
UserName : Enter HarryPotter
OrderID : Enter 20160630-12928
ReturnDate : Enter 20190705
UserAmount : Enter 142.23
Task 3 : Create Item
Now click on create item.
Navigate to the Tables and click on Index tab next to Overview section.
Click on Create Index.
Task 3 : Create Item
Enter the Index name as WhizIndex. Enter the Paritition key as ReturnDate and Sort key as UserAmount
Leave everything as default and Click on Create index and wait until the index state changes to Active.
Once the Global Secondary Index is active, you can check the Indexes tab to confirm. Note: It will take 5-10 minutes to be active.
Task 3 : Create Item

Click on Explore table Items tab and click on Create item. Insert the following additional data into the table and click on Create item. Attribute name - Attribute Value Enter UserName : Enter HarryPotter Enter OrderID : Enter 20160630-28176 Enter ReturnDate : Enter 20190513 Enter UserAmount : Enter 88.30 Attribute name - Attribute Value Enter UserName : Enter Ron Enter OrderID : Enter 20170609-25875 Enter ReturnDate : Enter 20190628 Enter UserAmount : Enter 116.86 Attribute name - Attribute Value Enter UserName : Enter Ron Enter OrderID : Enter 20170609-4177 Enter ReturnDate : Enter 20190731 Enter UserAmount : Enter 27.89 Attribute name - Attribute Value Enter UserName : Enter Voldemort Enter OrderID : Enter 20170609-17146 Enter ReturnDate : Enter 20190511 Enter UserAmount : Enter 114.00 Attribute name - Attribute Value Enter UserName : Enter Voldemort Enter OrderID : Enter 20170609-18618 Enter ReturnDate : Enter 20190615 Enter UserAmount : Enter 122.45
Click on Explore table Items tab and click on Create item. Insert the following additional data into the table and click on Create item.
Attribute name - Attribute Value Enter UserName : Enter HarryPotter Enter OrderID : Enter 20160630-28176 Enter ReturnDate : Enter 20190513 Enter UserAmount : Enter 88.30

Please note that, you need to create items for individual person. Do not add all the details of every person at once and create a single item.
The table looks something like this after creating all the items with provided data.
Task 3 : Create Item
Also you can edit the values if you have made any mistake while creating the items by selecting the item, editing it and saving the changes.

tasks.title: Task 4 : Use Global Secondary Index to Fetch Data

steps.text: In this task, you will utilize the Global Secondary Index (ReturnDate-UserAmount-index) to fetch data from the DynamoDB table named "WhizOrderTable." Follow the steps below:
Now go to Explore Table items and click on the Scan or Query option for WhizOrderTable.
Refresh the page before using the scan option to display the index.
Lets try with the Scan option to search for data.
Select the “Scan” option (which is in the left upper corner).
Table or index : Select Index - WhizIndex (You can select the Index that you've created)
Expand Filters, Attribute name : Enter ReturnDate Type : Select String Condition : Select Between Value : Enter 20190501 and 20190531
Expand Filters,
Attribute name : Enter ReturnDate
Type : Select String
Condition : Select Between
Value : Enter 20190501 and 20190531
In this example, we are trying to fetch the users who returned their orders in the month of May.
Now click on Run button.
Task 4 : Use Global Secondary Index to Fetch Data
Now you will be able to see the items returned below based on the search filters we have given.
Task 4 : Use Global Secondary Index to Fetch Data
Lets try with the Query option to search for some data. (Click on Remove before proceeding with Query option)

Click on Query next to Scan. Table or index : Select Index - WhizIndex (You can select the Index that you've created) ReturnDate : Enter 20190628 UserAmount (Sort key) : Select Greater than or equal to Enter Attribute Value : Enter 100
Click on Query next to Scan.
Table or index : Select Index - WhizIndex (You can select the Index that you've created)
ReturnDate : Enter 20190628
UserAmount (Sort key) : Select Greater than or equal to
Enter Attribute Value : Enter 100
Task 4 : Use Global Secondary Index to Fetch Data
Here we need to check which users returned an item on that particular day. To do so, we use the sort key to qualify the amount, as per the requirement.
Click on Run button.
Now you will be able to see the items returned below based on the search filters we have given.
Task 4 : Use Global Secondary Index to Fetch Data
This way global secondary index allows us to quickly find all the returns entered on various dates, which would normally require full table scans.
Once the lab steps are completed, please click on the Validate button on the left side panel.
This will validate the resources in the AWS account and displays whether you have completed this lab successfully or not.
Sample output :
You have created a DynamoDB table with Global Secondary Indexes.
You have successfully fetched data using Global Secondary Indexes.
Sign out of the AWS account.
You have successfully completed the lab.
Once you have completed the steps, click on End Lab from the lab console.
